source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from a through-truss bridge of the collegiate AISC-style type: top-chord HSS members, bottom-chord HSS members, diagonal web HSS members, vertical post HSS members, deck stringers / floor beams, transverse cross-frames, gusset plates at each welded joint, abutment pads at the two supports, and bolted splice plates at the member-length breaks required by the original sample's 1.2 m transportation limit. The original brief explicitly says the bridge 'shall be assembled from members no longer than 1.2 m' and that 'bolted splice locations must carry the full R1 load' — this forces a 20+ member decomposition.

full_prompt:
Design a single-piece structural-steel bridge truss for a collegiate bridge-design event. The bridge shall span a nominal 20 ft (6.10 m) clear gap and carry a maximum applied vertical load of 2,500 lb (11,120 N) over the decking at the worst-case loading location. Exactly two stringers carry the decking loads. Overall bridge envelope: 6.5 m long (to allow support bearings outside the 20 ft span), 1.0 m wide (stringer-to-stringer), 0.7 m tall. Bridge is a through-truss welded from A500 Grade B steel hollow structural sections (HSS). Material: ASTM A500 Grade B steel, E = 200 GPa, yield = 315 MPa, ultimate = 400 MPa, density = 7850 kg/m^3. Primary members: top chord HSS 50 x 50 x 3 mm, bottom chord HSS 50 x 50 x 3 mm, diagonal web HSS 40 x 40 x 3 mm, transverse floor beams HSS 40 x 40 x 3 mm. Nodes are welded (modeled as rigid connections). Load cases: (R1) Service load: 2500 lb (11,120 N) concentrated at mid-span on both stringers simultaneously; maximum midspan vertical deflection of the loaded stringers <= span/500 = 6100 / 500 = 12.2 mm. (R2) Stress pass/fail: under R1, maximum von Mises stress in any member <= yield / 1.67 (LRFD phi 0.9 / gamma 1.5 equivalent) = 189 MPa. (R3) Linear buckling: under factored load 1.5 x 11,120 = 16,680 N, the first buckling eigenvalue (compressive chord / diagonal buckling) shall be >= 2.5. (R4) Bridge total mass (stiffness / lightness scoring): aim for minimum; hard cap mass <= 120 kg. (R5) Lateral sway under a horizontal rubbing load of 50 lb (222 N) applied at mid-span top chord <= 12 mm. (R6) Construction constraint: the bridge shall be assembled from members no longer than 1.2 m (transportation constraint) - this is a geometry rule not analysis, but bolted splice locations must carry the full R1 load. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static) + linear buckling. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded assembly (treated as a single structural idealization for FEA), member schedule, and a report listing total mass, maximum midspan deflection, max von Mises stress, first buckling load factor, lateral sway.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The deliverable is reconfigured as a 20+ member welded through-truss assembly consistent with the original 6.5 m x 1.0 m x 0.7 m envelope and the explicit 1.2 m max member length (R6). Parts: top-chord HSS members, bottom-chord HSS members, diagonal web HSS members, vertical post HSS members, two deck stringers (the specifically named stringers that carry the 2500 lb service load), transverse cross-frames tying the two stringer lines, welded gusset plates at every truss node, two abutment pads (pin + roller supports), and bolted splice plates at every member-length break (required by R6). FEM verification runs on the assembled bridge only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Splice-plate bolt DCR, gusset-plate weld DCR, and abutment-pad reactions must be verified at assembled level. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design a structural-steel through-truss bridge for a collegiate bridge-design event as a multi-member welded + bolted assembly. The bridge spans a 20 ft (6.10 m) clear gap and carries a maximum applied vertical load of 2500 lb (11,120 N) over decking at the worst-case loading location, with exactly two stringers.

Overall envelope: 6.5 m long, 1.0 m wide, 0.7 m tall. Material: ASTM A500 Grade B steel (E = 200 GPa, yield = 315 MPa, ultimate = 400 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3). Primary members: top/bottom chord HSS 50 x 50 x 3 mm; diagonal web, verticals, floor beams HSS 40 x 40 x 3 mm. Welded nodes modeled as rigid.

LC1 service: 11,120 N concentrated at mid-span on both stringers; midspan vertical deflection <= L/500 = 12.2 mm. LC2 stress: max von Mises <= 189 MPa (315 / 1.67). LC3 linear buckling under factored 1.5 x 11,120 = 16,680 N: first eigenvalue >= 2.5. LC lateral: 50 lb (222 N) horizontal rubbing at mid-span top chord; sway <= 12 mm. Bridge mass <= 120 kg.

Construction constraint: members no longer than 1.2 m (transportation); bolted splice locations must carry the full R1 load.

prompt.title: Collegiate steel through-truss bridge assembly (20 ft clear span, 20+ members)
prompt.brief: HSS steel through-truss 20 ft clear span assembled from top/bottom chords + diagonals + verticals + stringers + cross-frames + gussets + abutment pads + bolted splice plates (required by max-1.2 m member rule R6). 2 stringers, 11.12 kN vertical load, deflection <= L/500, buckling factor >= 2.5.

load_cases.name: service_vertical
load_cases.direction: vertical at midspan on both stringers

boundary_conditions.mounting: pinned at one abutment pad, roller at the other (simple span)
boundary_conditions.loads: concentrated vertical at midspan on the two stringers; horizontal rubbing load at mid-span top chord

parts.name: top_chord_HSS_member
parts.role: longitudinal compression chord along the top of each side truss; individual member <= 1.2 m, spliced at intermediate panel points; primary compressive member governing R3 buckling
parts.count_note: Minimum 6 on a ~6.5 m bridge with <= 1.2 m member limit: 3 segments per side x 2 sides. Teams with more panels use more.

parts.name: bottom_chord_HSS_member
parts.role: longitudinal tension chord along the bottom of each side truss; individual member <= 1.2 m, spliced at intermediate panel points; carries the reaction from the two stringers

parts.name: diagonal_web_HSS_member
parts.role: diagonal web member that converts vertical panel shear into top-chord compression and bottom-chord tension; every panel of each side truss has at least one diagonal; all diagonals <= 1.2 m
parts.count_note: Pratt / Warren arrangement with 3 diagonals per side = 6 minimum; 8 is common.

parts.name: vertical_post_HSS_member
parts.role: vertical post at each panel point of each side truss; carries the panel reaction from the floor beam and stabilizes the top chord against out-of-plane buckling
parts.count_note: Minimum 2 per side (at midspan and one intermediate) = 4 total; many teams use 6-8.

parts.name: deck_stringer
parts.role: one of exactly two stringers that span the 6.10 m clear gap and receive the 11,120 N service load at midspan; welded / bolted to the cross-frames and transmits floor-beam reaction into the side trusses

parts.name: transverse_cross_frame
parts.role: transverse floor beam / cross-frame that ties the two stringer lines together at each panel point and carries the decking reaction into the two side trusses
parts.count_note: Minimum 4 (one at each side-truss panel point); more are typical.

parts.name: gusset_plate
parts.role: welded gusset plate at each truss node; develops the intersecting HSS member forces through fillet welds and is the governing location for R_asm weld DCR
parts.count_note: Minimum 8 (4 panel points per side x 2 sides); 12-16 is typical for a 3-panel through-truss.

geometric_constraints.weld_type: all-around fillet to each framing HSS

parts.name: abutment_pad
parts.role: end bearing pad at the pin or roller support; reacts the full 11,120 N vertical reaction at the abutment and provides the boundary condition for LC1-LC3
parts.count_note: Exactly 2 (pin + roller) per the simple-span idealization.
parts.material_hint: ASTM A572 Gr 50 plate + PTFE sliding layer on the roller end

parts.name: bolted_splice_plate
parts.role: double-lap splice plate pair at every chord / stringer / diagonal member-length break required by R6 (max member length 1.2 m); original brief states 'bolted splice locations must carry the full R1 load'; governs R_asm1 splice-bolt DCR
parts.count_note: Minimum 8 splice-plate pairs (4 per side for chord splices + deck-stringer splices). Teams with more member breaks use more.
parts.material_hint: ASTM A572 Gr 50 plate + A325 / 8.8 high-strength bolts

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X = span, Y = transverse, Z = vertical

pass_fail_criteria.description: Splice-plate bolt DCR at every bolted splice location (required by R6 member-length <= 1.2 m rule) shall be <= 1.0 under LC1 service load. Matches the original prompt: 'bolted splice locations must carry the full R1 load.'
Gusset-plate weld DCR at every truss-node gusset shall be <= 1.0 under LC1 service load and LC2 factored load.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (B32) for HSS members, shell for gusset plates, solid for abutment pads and splice plates

interfaces.mediated_by: fillet weld from each framing HSS onto the gusset
interfaces.load_path: intersecting-member forces at each upper panel point develop into the gusset

interfaces.mediated_by: fillet weld from each framing HSS onto the gusset
interfaces.load_path: intersecting-member forces at each lower panel point develop into the gusset

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M16 Grade 8.8 bolts in double-lap shear at each splice
interfaces.load_path: full member axial + moment continuity across the 1.2 m member-length break required by R6; must carry LC1 per the original prompt

interfaces.mediated_by: fillet weld at each panel point
interfaces.load_path: stringer reaction into cross-frame and then into the two side trusses

interfaces.mediated_by: fillet weld of pin detail (one end) or sliding PTFE face on roller (other end)
interfaces.load_path: full reaction from simple-span idealization (pin + roller) into the external supports

fem_check_policy.rationale: R1-R5 are whole-bridge checks (midspan deflection, max member stress across all members, first buckling eigenvalue, total mass, lateral sway); R6 forces a multi-member decomposition via the 1.2 m member rule. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The original prompt explicitly requires member length <= 1.2 m on a 6.5 m bridge (R6), which mandates bolted splices, and says 'bolted splice locations must carry the full R1 load' — making the bridge inherently a 20+ member welded + bolted assembly, not a single idealized truss.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact panel count, gusset plan dimensions, splice-plate bolt count, and abutment-pad footprint are speculative; HSS sizes, span, max-member-length rule, and load magnitudes are anchored to the original sample.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
